markdownRemark.html:
As a React-based component library, Choerodon-ui requires its data handling unique keys to also comply with React's key configuration rules. Components in the library such as Table, Select, and Tree, which render components at the same level, need to pay special attention to this rule to avoid unexpected UI display bugs.

In addition to using the library components, it is also recommended to associate unique keys when dealing with arrays of elements in your own development, as this helps with performance.

Key Generation Rules Inside Components

When component library components are used in conjunction with DataSet, Record.key is used as the default unique identifier during rendering.

@computed
get key(): string | number {
  if (!this.isNew) {
    const { primaryKey } = this.dataSet.props;
    if (primaryKey) {
      const key = this.get(primaryKey);
      if (isString(key) || isNumber(key)) {
        return key;
      }
    }
  }
  return this.id;
}

If there are data issues or error messages related to the key, you can troubleshoot them according to the following key generation rules.

Select

Select.Option combines and concatenates the value list's value field and text field with Record.id.

export function getItemKey(record: Record, text: ReactNode, value: any) {
  return `item-${value || record.id}-${(isValidElement(text) ? text.key : text) || record.id}`;
}
const value = record.get(valueField);
const text = record.get(textField);
const key: Key = getItemKey(record, text, value);

If you want to customize the corresponding concatenated content, you can configure textField and valueField at the Field.Props level.

Table

The Table.Row component uses Record.id as Record.key by default.

Users can use primaryKey at the DataSet.Props level to set a custom data field as the unique identifier for the Record.

// Get table column key
const { key: rowKey } = record;

// Table Column Rendering
const tr = (
  <Element
    key={rowKey}
    {...rowExternalProps}
    {...rowProps}
    style={rowStyle}
  >
    {columns}
  </Element>
);

Lov

Lov supports both dropdown selection mode and popup modal mode.

Dropdown Selection Mode

Its key generation rules are the same as Select.

Popup Modal Mode

The Lov data will be displayed in the form of Modal + Table.

const valueField = field ? field.get('valueField', record) : config.valueField; // config refers to the data returned by the LOV view configuration
const dsProps: DataSetProps = {
  transport: {
    read: this.getQueryAxiosConfig(code, field, config, record),
  },
  primaryKey: valueField,
  cacheSelection: true,
};

You can customize the valueField and modify the unique key for popup mode data through three approaches: valueField in Value Set View Configuration, valueField at the Field.Props level, and changing primaryKey in optionsProps.

Priority of the Three Approaches

valueField in Value Set View Configuration < valueField at the Field.Props level < primaryKey modified in optionsProps; essentially, all of them modify the primaryKey property of the Table's DataSet.

Tree

When rendering the Tree.TreeNode component, its unique identifier is determined by Record.key, DataSet.primaryKey, and DataSet.idField (with increasing priority).

export function getKey(record, idField) {
  return String(idField ? record.get(idField) : record.key);
}

Common Issues

Mismatch Between Rendered Data Volume and Total Count

Tree

Take the Tree component as an example. At the DateSet.Props level, idField is configured as id (which will be used as the key during rendering).

The total number of records returned by the backend is twelve (2 parent nodes, 10 child nodes), and the normal rendering result is as follows:

At this point, there are no duplicate ids among parent and child nodes. If id duplicates occur, for example, in the backend data where "Report Definition" and "Report Management" have the same id, the rendering result will be as follows:

There are duplicate id values, which causes the child data under that id to fail to associate correctly, and as a result, the associated data cannot be rendered.

Tabs

When the key configured in Tabs.TabPane is duplicated, the number of rendered tabs will be fewer than the actual number written.

Multiple child items highlighting simultaneously when selecting Menu

When the key configured in Menu.Item is duplicated, selecting a Menu can cause multiple Menu.Item elements with the same key to be highlighted at the same time.

Solution: Check whether the key configuration of Menu.Item is duplicated.

Abnormal jumping issue in Select

The Options inside the Select component reuse the Menu and its subsidiary MenuItem from rc-components. When the mouseEnter event of a MenuItem is triggered, it marks this DOM element as active. The component internally listens for changes in the active state and performs scrollIntoView() on the corresponding MenuItem to scroll it into view.

When two or more identical pieces of data exist, because there are multiple DOM elements with the same key value, all of them trigger scrollIntoView(), which causes a problem where the content in the Select dropdown jumps abnormally.

When collapsing a single Panel, other Panels are also affected

Reason: The key configured for Collapse.Panel is duplicated.
